judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit every expectation; Report 1 is marginally stronger because it explicitly frames doc-code drift as a bug and endorses same-commit doc updates as a going-forward norm, but the two are essentially comparable.

report_1.evidence: Refund calls should live in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py`. ... `backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14` — the charge route builds its own `chargekit.Client` ... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9` — the dunning worker constructs a fresh `chargekit.Client` inside its loop ... Those are the only two; `gateway.py` and `errors.py` are the legitimate sites.

report_2.evidence: New provider calls should go in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py` ... with `billing/errors.py` as the only sanctioned exception (it imports only exception types, `errors.py:6-9`) ... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` ... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13` ... Those are the only violations

report_1.evidence: `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:11-12` says the response contains *exactly* `id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status`. The actual response model `InvoiceOut` (`backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`) also includes `external_ref`, which the charge route returns (`invoices.py:17-18`).

report_2.evidence: The actual response model `InvoiceOut` (`models/invoice.py:16-22`) includes a sixth field, `external_ref`, populated by the charge endpoint (`api/invoices.py:17`) and backed by migration `backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`.

report_1.evidence: `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` should be corrected **in the same commit as whatever code change resolves each discrepancy** — drift between the contract doc and the code is a bug, not a "fix later" item. ... the doc fix (plus, ideally, restoring the boundary) is the first commit of this feature, and the refund accessor lands with a same-commit doc update describing it.

report_2.evidence: It should be updated as part of the refund feature work — before or alongside the first refund PR, not after — since it's the document the README (`medibill/README.md:4`) directs engineers to, and every one of its three claims is currently stale or false.

report_1.evidence: The code (`backend/app/api/webhooks.py:10-19`) handles **five** ... I would drop the hardcoded count entirely — a stated number like "4" is guaranteed to drift (and already has). Restate it as either "the handled event types are enumerated in `backend/app/api/webhooks.py`" (point at the single source of truth)

report_2.evidence: The code at `backend/app/api/webhooks.py:10-19` handles **5** ... stop hard-coding an exact count ("4 webhook event types") in prose — either enumerate the types without a count, or point to `app/api/webhooks.py` as the source of truth, since exact-count claims silently rot every time a handler is added
